alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework developed for RCOMP-SSC is scalable for other data point selection strategies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The control matrix developed for RCOMP-SSC can be applied to other selection strategies for data points.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same idea about applying the control mechanism to other selection strategies with equivalent intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate the superiority of RCOMP-SSC compared to other clustering methods in terms of accuracy and computational time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on synthetic data and real-world databases demonstrate that RCOMP-SSC outperforms other methods in clustering accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the experimental superiority claim but emphasizes accuracy (and specifies datasets); the explicit claim about computational time is de-emphasized, narrowing the scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Certain sparse DNNs can train to the same precision as dense DNNs at lower runtime and storage cost.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Certain sparse DNNs can train to the same precision as dense DNNs at lower runtime and storage cost.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the experimental finding directly with the same claims about precision and efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RadiX-Nets are sparse DNN topologies that are much more diverse than X-Net topologies while preserving X-Nets' desired characteristics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RadiX-Nets are sparse DNN topologies that are much more diverse than X-Net topologies while preserving their desired characteristics.
alignments.rationale: The characterization of RadiX-Nets is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sparse neural network topologies can attain the same expressive power as dense counterparts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sparse neural network topologies can attain the same expressive power as dense counterparts.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the theoretical/conceptual conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: XDream is an efficient algorithm for uncovering neuronal tuning preferences in black-box networks using a vast and diverse stimulus space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: XDream is an efficient, general, and robust algorithm for uncovering neuronal tuning preferences using a vast and diverse stimulus space.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the main conclusion about XDream's efficiency and generality with the same emphasis on a diverse stimulus space.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: XDream can reliably create strong stimuli for neurons in the macaque visual cortex.
alignments.pub_claim_text: XDream can efficiently find preferred features for visual units without any prior knowledge about them.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the experimental macaque result to 'visual units' broadly and frames it as efficient discovery without prior knowledge, broadening the scope beyond the specific species.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: XDream is applicable across network layers, architectures, and training sets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: XDream extrapolates to different layers, architectures, and developmental regimes, performing better than brute-force search.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the extrapolation across layers and architectures and adds a comparative performance claim, which amplifies the original applicability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A method for gradient-free activation maximization was developed by combining a generative neural network with a genetic algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: XDream is robust to choices of multiple image generators, optimization algorithms, and hyperparameters, suggesting that its performance is locally near-optimal.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on the robustness and near-optimality of the method (including generators and optimizers), which clarifies and emphasizes properties of the developed gradient-free combination rather than restating the implementation detail.

alignments.pub_claim_text: There is no significant advantage to problem-specific parameter tuning when using XDream.
alignments.rationale: This claim about parameter tuning does not appear among the preprint claims and is newly asserted in the publication, so it is recorded as an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI can support the achievement of 128 targets across all SDGs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI can enable the accomplishment of 134 targets across all the Sustainable Development Goals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about AI enabling SDG targets is preserved but the numeric count changed (128 -> 134), so the result was revised while scope and confidence remain similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI may inhibit 58 targets of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI may inhibit 59 targets related to the Sustainable Development Goals. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The inhibitory-targets finding is retained but the numeric value changed (58 -> 59) and the publication expresses lower certainty, so the claim was revised and confidence tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI may lead to increased inequalities among and within countries, hindering the achievement of the 2030 Agenda. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's specific conclusion about AI-driven inequalities is not reflected in the publication's listed claims and therefore appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fast development of AI needs to be supported by appropriate policy and regulation to avoid gaps in transparency, accountability, safety, and ethical standards. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fast development of AI needs to be supported by necessary regulatory insight and oversight. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a lack of research assessing the medium- and long-term impacts of AI. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.rationale: The publication retains the limitation about research gaps but expresses it more broadly (overlooked aspects of impact rather than only medium/long-term), thus broadening the scope of the original limitation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Failure to provide regulatory oversight for AI could result in gaps in transparency, safety, and ethical standards. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit consequence statement about failure of oversight causing gaps in standards that was not present as a distinct claim in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Recombinator-$k$-means is generally superior in terms of the optimization objective compared to a standard genetic algorithm with deterministic pairwise-nearest-neighbor crossover. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: For short times, the (augmented) pairwise-nearest-neighbor method is always superior to recombinator-$k$-means when population sizes are adjusted to match running times. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantum generative adversarial learning (QuGAL) has exponential advantages over classical adversarial networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not claim exponential advantages for QuGAL over classical adversarial networks; that claim is absent or not supported in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quantum multiplicative matrix weight algorithm (QMMW) is proposed as the first quantum generative adversarial learning algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose an efficient quantum adversarial bipartite entanglement detection scheme.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's specific claim of QMMW as the first QuGAL algorithm does not appear identically; the publication focuses on an entanglement detection scheme rather than presenting the original QMMW claim, so the correspondence is weak and the claim is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational complexity of QMMW is polynomially proportional to the number of training rounds and logarithmically proportional to the input size.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The runtime complexity of our proposal is O(poly(N)T) for an N-qubit quantum state.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the runtime bound in a concrete O(.) form consistent with the preprint's complexity description, clarifying the complexity statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm combines QuGAL with online learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not describe combining QuGAL with online learning; this methodological claim is absent in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical experiments for the task of entanglement test for pure state support the claims made in this paper.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We experimentally implement our protocol on a linear optical network and demonstrate its effectiveness for bipartite entanglement detection.
alignments.rationale: The preprint reported numerical experiments, whereas the publication reports an actual experimental implementation on a linear optical network, which strengthens and broadens the validation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed grid spectral mixture (GSM) kernel can approximate the underlying stationary kernel arbitrarily close.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed grid spectral mixture (GSM) kernel closely approximates the underlying stationary kernel for GP regression in time series modeling.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the approximation claim but situates it specifically for GP regression in time series modeling, narrowing the application context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The maximum-likelihood estimation framework is used to estimate the unknown GP hyper-parameters, including the non-negative weights of all sub-kernels and the noise variance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The maximum-likelihood estimation method is used to determine the unknown GP hyper-parameters, including nonnegative weights of sub-kernels and noise variance.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same MLE usage for estimating GP hyper-parameters; the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sequential majorization-minimization (MM) method is an efficient solver that matches the low-rank property of the proposed GSM sub-kernels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two optimization methods, including a sequential majorization-minimization method and a nonlinearly constrained alternating direction method of multipliers, are introduced for solving the unknown hyper-parameters.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the sequential MM method alongside ADMM as part of a pair of introduced optimization methods, broadening the optimization-solver presentation relative to the preprint's MM-focused claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The non-linearly constrained alternating direction of multiplier method (ADMM) has the potential to generate better local minima in terms of test mean-squared-error (MSE).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two optimization methods, including a sequential majorization-minimization method and a nonlinearly constrained alternating direction method of multipliers, are introduced for solving the unknown hyper-parameters.
alignments.rationale: The preprint made an explicit performance claim about ADMM yielding better local minima; the publication lists ADMM as an introduced method but does not assert the stronger performance benefit, thus tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed GSM kernel-based GP regression model outperforms several competitors in terms of prediction mean-squared-error and numerical stability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GSM kernel-based GP regression model outperforms several benchmarks in terms of prediction accuracy and numerical stability.
alignments.rationale: Both versions claim the GSM-based GP regression outperforms competitors on prediction accuracy (MSE) and numerical stability; the publication restates this result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Boundaries of PICO span annotations by individual human annotators are very diverse. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Boundaries of PICO span annotations by individual annotators are very diverse. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are effectively identical in wording and certainty, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Despite the disagreement in span boundaries, general areas of the span annotations are broadly agreed by annotators. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Despite the disagreement in span boundaries, general areas of the span annotations are broadly agreed by annotators. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is repeated verbatim with the same level of certainty; no change in meaning or scope is observed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applying a standard agreement alone may undermine the agreement of PICO spans. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applying a standard agreement alone may undermine the agreement of PICO spans. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint claim with identical content and certainty, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adopting both a standard and a relaxed agreements is more suitable for PICO span evaluation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adopting both a standard and a relaxed agreements is more suitable for PICO span evaluation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim appears verbatim in the publication with the same conclusion and certainty, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Low-frequency concepts in the medical domain exhibit the same high stability as high-frequency concepts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Low-frequency concepts in the medical domain exhibit the same high stability as high-frequency concepts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The statement is identical between preprint and publication, preserving meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The noisiness of context words influences the stability of medical concept embeddings, regardless of frequency. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The noisiness of context words influences the stability of medical concept embeddings regardless of frequency. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a linear correlation between the noisiness of context words and the stability of medical concept embeddings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a linear correlation between the noisiness of context words and the stability of medical concept embeddings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over unchanged and retains the same level of confidence and specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed factor of context word noisiness extends to the stability of word embeddings in the general domain. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The linear relation of the proposed factor extends to the stability of word embeddings in the general domain. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that it is the linear relation that extends to general-domain embeddings and raises the certainty from medium to high, a clearer and stronger formulation of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed 3-gated model effectively fuses global and local image features for image caption generation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed 3-gated model effectively fuses global and local image features for image caption generation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated verbatim in the publication with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first gate in the model adaptively controls the integration of global image features into the sentence generator. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The first gate in the model adaptively controls the integration of global image features into the sentence generator. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this result exactly, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of a gated recurrent neural network (RNN) as the sentence generator enhances the model's performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of a gated recurrent neural network (RNN) as the sentence generator enhances the model's performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim and its certainty remain the same between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art for image caption generation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art for image caption generation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental result is identically reported in the publication with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Siamese Attentional Keypoint Network (SATIN) demonstrates strong capability to learn more effective object representations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new Siamese lightweight hourglass network captures more global and local contextual information at multiple scales. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SATIN tracker achieves state-of-the-art performance on several recent benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Siamese Attentional Keypoint Network (SATIN) achieves state-of-the-art performance on several recent benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel cross-attentional module enhances both discriminative and localization capabilities of feature maps. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel cross-attentional module enhances both discriminative and localization capabilities of feature maps. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: SATIN is computationally and memory storage efficient during both training and testing stages. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Orthogonally Regularized Deep SR network (ORDSR) achieves state-of-the-art Super-Resolution image quality with fewer parameters than most deep CNN methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Orthogonally Regularized Deep SR network (ORDSR) achieves state-of-the-art super-resolution image quality with fewer parameters than most deep CNN methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ORDSR successfully overcomes the artifacts introduced by bicubic interpolation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ORDSR successfully overcomes the artifacts introduced by bicubic interpolation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ORDSR exhibits a more graceful degradation in performance as the training size is reduced, providing significant benefits in scenarios with limited training data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ORDSR exhibits a more graceful degradation in performance as the training size is reduced, providing significant benefits in scenarios with limited training data. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Analysis of memory and computation requirements indicates that ORDSR allows for a more efficient network with faster inference. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of the Convolutional DCT (CDCT) layer into the network structure simplifies the super-resolution task by leveraging the image transform domain. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning-based location estimators, such as deep neural networks, demonstrate high-precision localization performance by automatically learning discriminative features from noisy wireless signal measurements. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The inner workings of deep neural networks in indoor localization applications are not transparent and not adequately understood. (certainty=high, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed visualization techniques provide quantitative and visual explanations for the deep neural network learning process and the critical features learned during this process. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Dimensionality reduction visualization can project high-dimensional feature space to 2D space to facilitate visualization and interpretation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed portfolio frameworks significantly outperform any single algorithm on the set of benchmark problems. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Two portfolio frameworks for surrogate-assisted evolutionary algorithms are proposed to reduce the risk of choosing an inappropriate algorithm for computationally expensive problems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The UCB-IBSAEA framework employs the Upper Confidence Bound policy from reinforcement learning to select the most appropriate algorithm at each iteration. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the benefits of using state-of-the-art machine learning methods in the analysis of historical photo archives. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We were able to find some special characteristics for different photographers in terms of their typical photo content and framing. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We managed to train a neural network that can successfully recognize the photographer from some of the photos. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A local randomization approach that is differentially private is adopted to provide users with a self-controlled privacy guarantee for the most sensitive information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A local randomization approach that is differentially private is adopted to provide users with a self-controlled privacy guarantee for the most sensitive information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The local differential privacy mechanism is described the same way in both versions and retains its claimed guarantees.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ADMM algorithm is perturbed through a combined noise-adding method to enhance privacy protection for both sensitive and less sensitive information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ADMM algorithm is perturbed through a combined noise-adding method to enhance privacy protection for both sensitive and less sensitive information. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The mechanism description is unchanged, but the publication reports a lower certainty (high -> medium), so the claim's confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments using real-world datasets validate the theoretical results and evaluate the classification performance of the proposed framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments using real-world datasets validate the theoretical results and evaluate the classification performance of the proposed framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental validation on real-world datasets is retained in the publication with the same characterization and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RuleKit is a versatile tool for rule learning that combines interpretability with predictive power. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RuleKit is a versatile tool for rule learning that combines interpretability with predictive power. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core claim about RuleKit's combination of interpretability and predictive power is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RuleKit is suitable for classification, regression, and survival problems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RuleKit is suitable for classification, regression, and survival problems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The stated applicability to these problem types is preserved exactly in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The user-guided induction in RuleKit facilitates verifying hypotheses concerning data dependencies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The user-guided induction in RuleKit facilitates verifying hypotheses concerning data dependencies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the user-guided induction claim with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RuleKit allows straightforward investigation of different induction schemes in a powerful and flexible experimental environment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RuleKit allows straightforward investigation of different induction schemes in a powerful and flexible experimental environment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The description of the experimental environment and its flexibility is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RuleKit is publicly available on GitHub under the GNU AGPL-3.0 license. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the statement about GitHub availability and license present in the preprint, so this distribution claim was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The designed three-qubit flux-tunable controlled-controlled-phase gate achieves a fidelity of over 99.99%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine-learning techniques designed three-qubit entangling gates with fidelities of >99.9% and a duration of 50 ns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The fidelity claim in the publication is weakened from >99.99% to >99.9% and additional details (duration) are given, so the quantitative performance claim was revised and tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The gate design procedure incorporates realistic constraints and analyzes robustness under decoherence, distortion, and random noise. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The published paper does not explicitly restate the claimed robustness analysis under those specific realistic constraints, indicating this procedural claim was removed or de-emphasized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The controlled-controlled-phase gate, in combination with two single-qubit gates, successfully realizes a Toffoli gate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The controlled-controlled-phase gate combined with two single-qubit gates realizes a Toffoli gate, which is widely used in quantum circuits. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The realization of a Toffoli gate from the described gate plus single-qubit gates is preserved verbatim, with consistent certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A three-qubit entangling Parity Checker gate was introduced, which has applications in quantum arithmetic circuits and quantum error correction schemes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This Parity Checker gate and its applications do not appear in the preprint claims and therefore represent an added contribution in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The designed circuit for Shor's nine-qubit quantum error correction code was compared to conventional realizations. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparison of a circuit for Shor's nine-qubit code is not present in the preprint claim set, indicating this result was added in the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Advances in neural network-trained word embeddings empower non-embodied text-processing algorithms and suggest similar utility for embodied machines. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Advances in neural network-trained word embeddings can empower embodied machines to respond to natural language commands. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's suggestion into a clearer statement about empowering embodied machines; the core idea is the same but more directly stated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training robots to act similarly to semantically-similar word2vec encoded commands enables them to act appropriately to previously-unheard commands. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method introduced allows robots to act appropriately to previously-unheard commands after training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental result that robots generalize to previously-unheard commands after training is preserved with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mechanical structure of the robot can facilitate or hinder the alignment between motoric and linguistic similarities. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The alignment between motoric and linguistic similarities in robots can be facilitated or hindered by the mechanical structure of the robot. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The statement about the role of robot mechanical structure in alignment is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inducing alignment between action, language, and robot structure points to future large scale methods. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest future methods that exploit relationships between action, language, and robot structure. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions present this as a forward-looking suggestion about exploiting action-language-structure relationships; the publication wording is slightly different but the claim is unchanged.
